extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out implies not sent) holds; the state (opted_out=true, sent=true) is unreachable. Reasoning: (1) opted_out starts false and the ONLY transition that sets it true is t1 (on optout). Nothing ever sets opted_out back to false, so once true it stays true. (2) t1 simultaneously sets sent:=false in the same update, so at the exact step opted_out becomes true, sent is false. (3) The ONLY transition that sets sent:=true is t2 (send_attempt), whose guard is 'not opted_out'. Therefore t2 is disabled whenever opted_out is true and can never set sent=true after opt-out. (4) Every other transition (t3-t9, including the key_* events and t9 send_attempt-while-opted-out) either sets sent:=false or leaves sent unchanged. Hence, after opted_out becomes true, sent is false and can only be re-cleared to false or left unchanged — never raised to true. Events are processed one at a time, so there is no same-step race allowing send_attempt to raise sent in the step that opt-out occurs. Consequently no reachable state has opted_out=true and sent=true, and the invariant holds across all executions (including the 5-event bound).
